old_claim.note: Paper Making provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped anchor artifact is a paper manuscript, making paper production an enabling medium for this surviving register tradition.

source_shape.source_locator: The State Archive of Genoa object page identifies the register as a paper manuscript and the oldest paper code in Western Europe.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source directly identifies the anchor notarial register as a paper manuscript.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because this node is anchored to the surviving Giovanni Scriba register, paper is an enabling material for the scoped register artifact.

ontology_before: Notarial records depended on paper making through a generic expert-inference edge supported only by a broad papermaking source.
ontology_after: Medieval notarial registers keep paper making as an enabling material for the source-backed Genoese paper register.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: paper_making remains a direct enabling prerequisite, now tied to the anchor artifact.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to all notarial acts including parchment and papyrus documents.
The source did not identify the anchor register as paper.
Paper making were treated as a hard prerequisite for all notarial practice.
short_reason: The anchor notarial register is explicitly a paper manuscript.
